npc: Jordan (VP Analytics) — data-driven, skeptical of conclusions without evidence, respects analysts who challenge their own assumptions

scene_1.title: WAU Dashboard Drop
scene_1.description: Weekly active users dropped 38% overnight. Jordan flagged it urgently. Student must investigate: tracking error or real business problem?

investigation_paths.path_1_tracking_error: Check if analytics JS snippet was removed in last deployment
investigation_paths.path_2_real_drop: Check if new feature rollout caused user confusion
investigation_paths.path_3_data_pipeline: Check if ETL job failed or ran late

scene_1.correct_first_step: Check data pipeline and tracking before assuming real business problem

scene_2.title: Root Cause Analysis
scene_2.description: Drop confirmed real. Student cleans messy dataset and identifies root cause across 3 possible explanations.
scene_2.trap: Feature rollout is correlated but not causal — holiday week explains the drop

scene_3.title: Findings Presentation to Jordan
scene_3.description: Present findings to skeptical VP. Jordan pushes back on methodology and asks about confounding variables.
jordan_pushback_questions: How do you know the holiday caused it and not the feature rollout?
Could there be a third variable you haven't considered?

scene_4.title: Intervention Recommendation
scene_4.description: Jordan asks for a specific, measurable recommendation based on findings. FINAL SCENE.
